response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but the fit for this specific role is weak. Professional C++ experience is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. There is no Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets systems experience — RIDE Capital was retail-investment fintech on a Node/TypeScript stack, not investment banking. SQL exposure is listed generically (MySQL, MongoDB) rather than SQL Server or Oracle. Strengths in testing culture, CI/CD, and systems programming do not offset the missing core requirements.
strengths: Extensive overall engineering experience (15+ years) across backend, systems, and low-latency work
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and DevOps tooling
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~6 months professionally in 2014–2015), versus a mandatory 5–10 years
No Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain experience
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; recent stack is TypeScript/Node/MongoDB

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth for mandatory requirement
Strong general engineering fundamentals and CI/CD background
